strengths: Nearly 20 years of engineering experience spanning web, mobile, desktop, embedded, and high-scale distributed systems (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second), comfortably exceeding the 15-year requirement

Current hands-on AI work at re:cinq building AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic-workflow frameworks — direct, practical fluency in the AI-powered product development this role centers on

Proven entrepreneurial and thought-leadership track record: founder of the open-source GISCollective platform (2016–present, adopted by Green Map) plus international conference talks and multi-day workshops, matching the role's mandate to inspire and advise others
concerns: No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, which the role lists as preferred
Career titles top out at Senior/Staff engineer; director-level strategic scope is implied by breadth and founding experience rather than explicitly demonstrated
Dedicated AI/agent work is recent (since April 2025), so recognized, at-scale AI products or platforms are not yet evidenced on the resume

key_factors.factor: Engineering depth and breadth (~20 years, full stack to embedded and high-performance systems)
Hands-on AI-agent and MCP infrastructure experience aligned with AI strategy focus
Founder experience and public technical leadership (talks, workshops, open source)

response.justification: This candidate pairs the required 15+ years of engineering experience with unusual range — from embedded systems and low-latency micro-services at massive scale to modern TypeScript/Node cloud products deployed on Kubernetes and AWS — exactly the builder-level code and design fluency the role prefers. His current work on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP tooling, and agentic frameworks puts him at the practical frontier of the AI focus areas, while founding and sustaining GISCollective demonstrates the entrepreneurial instinct and product ownership the role calls for within larger strategic contexts. A long history of workshops, conference talks, and mentoring shows he can advise partners and inspire teams through difficult technical change, making him a credible fit for a director-level IC charged with identifying and testing strategic AI differentiation.
